rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference component ports.
Use implementation scopes for bindings and assignments that are valid only at the implementation level.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid AADL scopes based on whether they reference component ports or implementation details.
Only include assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports or features.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Preserve AADL architecture by placing AGREE annexes in appropriate scopes without altering component structure.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations related to ports.
Implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments that are valid in that scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless they are valid in that context.
Avoid referencing subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
